mission.title: cri-o: Container output not being logged with CRI format when using runtime_type=vm
mission.description: Container output not being logged with CRI format when using runtime_type=vm. Requested by 11+ users.

steps.title: Check current cri-o deployment
steps.description:
Verify your cri-o version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cri-o -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cri-o
helm list -n cri-o 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working cri-o installation.

steps.title: Review cri-o configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant cri-o configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n cri-o -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cri-o
kubectl get configmap -n cri-o -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cri-o
```
**Description**

Logs from a container launched with `runtime_type=vm` (e.g. Kata, using the containerd shim) aren't formatted correctly. They're completely missing the CRI formatting (timestamp and stream).

steps.title: Apply the fix for Container output not being logged with CRI format when…
steps.description:
ze" : "1G",
        "numjobs" : "1",
        "runtime" : "60s",
        "fallocate" : "none",
        "invalidate" : "1"
      },
...
```

Although this is exactly what we'd expect as the output of `kubectl logs $pod`, the format in question will lead to the following error:
`failed to get parse
```yaml
vmss-agent-kata1-test-rmuuc000000 ~ # crio --version
crio version 1.17.4
commit: "39c8cdc95bc9f70635d756a3001416e7a3ceb1e1"
```

steps.title: Upgrade cri-o to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade cri-o:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade cri-o cri-o/cri-o --namespace cri-o
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cri-o
helm list -n cri-o
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cri-o -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cri-o
kubectl get events -n cri-o --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Container output not being logged with CRI format when…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
ze" : "1G",
        "numjobs" : "1",
        "runtime" : "60s",
        "fallocate" : "none",
        "invalidate" : "1"
      },
...
```

Although this is exactly what we'd expect as the output of `kubectl logs $pod`, the format in question will lead to the following error:
`failed to get parse function: unsupported log format: "{\n"`

After

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with cri-o installed or the issue environment reproducible.
